notes.anchor: Your letter has been wonderfully welcome to the Senate
notes.note: Plancus, as governor of Transalpine Gaul, reported officially to the Senate by despatch (litterae); such a despatch from a provincial commander would be read aloud in session and could itself be the trigger for honors voted to its author.

notes.anchor: whoever crushes Antony will have brought this most foul and most dangerous war to its end
notes.note: After the relief of Mutina (April 43) Antony had retreated across the Alps toward Lepidus's province; Cicero presses Plancus to be the man who finishes him off, the recurring refrain of his book-10 letters.
